description: secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs:447-491 put() calls nvm_overwrite, whose contract returns Error::DataLost { id } when the delete-then-add sequence fails after the delete succeeds (data is gone, caller may want to recreate or alarm). backend_err() at line 169 wraps this in Box<dyn Error> as SecretError::Backend, so the caller cannot recover the id or distinguish 'data is gone' from 'add failed but old object intact'. The wolfhsm crate intentionally introduced DataLost as a distinct variant (wolfhsm-0.1.0/src/error.rs:51-59); we discard the structure it provides. Trigger: server-side write quota hit or transport drops between nvm_delete and wh_Client_NvmAddObject inside nvm_overwrite -> caller gets Backend('wolfHSM error -2105 (NOSPACE)') with no programmatic way to learn that id was deleted. Fix: match on wolfhsm::Error::DataLost in put()'s error path and return a structured SecretError variant or at minimum log/document the lost id explicitly. Consider proposing a SecretError::DataLost variant in secretx-core if other backends have the same condition.
title: Error::DataLost flattened to opaque Backend; caller loses lost-id signal needed for recovery
